answered.question: Preset storage strategy?
answered.answer: JSON files in cli/presets/ directory. Node.js CLI project in cli/ subdirectory with own package.json. Dockerfile copies and npm links it globally.

answered.question: Application semantics: merge vs replace?
answered.answer: Deep merge (additive). Presets merge into existing settings. Stackable with last-wins on conflicts. Tracked state with per-preset removal support.

answered.question: CLI integration model?
answered.answer: Command name: claude-preset. Both interactive TUI (bare command) and direct CLI (claude-preset apply <name>). On-demand only, no auto-apply.

answered.question: Configuration surface?
answered.answer: Full surface: settings, permissions, MCP servers, skills, plugins, commands, subagents, CLAUDE.md sections (via @.claude/presets/<name>.md includes). Auto-install on apply.

answered.question: Initial preset list for v1?
answered.answer: NextJS + Convex. Start with 2-3, expand later.

answered.question: Test framework for install.sh?
answered.answer: ShellCheck + raw shell assertions. Zero external dependencies. Two-tier: pure logic tests always, Docker integration gated on DOCKER_AVAILABLE.

answered.question: CI pipeline design?
answered.answer: GitHub Actions. Single workflow with jobs: lint (ShellCheck), test (unit tests), security (hadolint + security checks). No container build in CI.

answered.question: Security audit scope?
answered.answer: Automated checks only. ShellCheck security rules, hadolint, verify SYS_ADMIN blocked, .devcontainer/ read-only, deny rules in settings.json.

answered.question: Test coverage boundaries?
answered.answer: Critical paths only: extract_mounts_to_file, cmd_sync, discover_resources, check_no_sys_admin, arg parsing. Minimal refactoring with source guard.

inferred.text: CLI tool is a Node.js project in cli/ with npm packages, not a bash script
Presets support deep merge with state tracking for undo/removal
CLAUDE.md preset content uses @include pattern from separate files

deferred.idea: Container build verification in CI (devcontainer build as a CI step)
Additional presets: Postgres, Expo, general-dev, minimal
Auto-apply presets via CLAUDE_PRESET env var
Preset composition (named combos like fullstack = nextjs + postgres)
